title: 3 Days in Rome: Ancient Wonders & Italian Flavors

stops.name: Colosseum
stops.notes: Start early to beat the crowds. Skip-the-line ticket recommended. Combo ticket includes Roman Forum and Palatine Hill.

stops.name: Roman Forum
stops.notes: Walk through the heart of ancient Rome. Wear comfortable shoes; terrain is uneven. Audio guide highly recommended.

stops.name: Roscioli Salumeria con Cucina
stops.notes: Iconic Roman trattoria famous for cacio e pepe and carbonara. Reservations essential, especially for dinner.

stops.name: Vatican Museums & Sistine Chapel
stops.notes: Book the earliest entry slot. Dress code enforced: shoulders and knees covered. Don't miss the Raphael Rooms.

stops.name: St. Peter's Basilica
stops.notes: Free entry but expect security lines. Climb the dome for panoramic views of Rome (extra fee, 551 steps or partial lift).

stops.name: Hotel Artemide
stops.notes: 4-star hotel near Termini Station. Excellent breakfast buffet and rooftop terrace. Walking distance to many attractions.

stops.name: Trevi Fountain
stops.notes: Visit early morning (before 8 AM) to avoid massive crowds. Toss a coin over your left shoulder for a return trip to Rome.

stops.name: Pantheon
stops.notes: Free entry but timed-ticket required on weekends. Best-preserved Roman building with an oculus open to the sky.

stops.name: Armando al Pantheon
stops.notes: Family-run trattoria steps from the Pantheon. Try the saltimbocca alla romana and tonnarelli cacio e pepe. Book weeks ahead.
